info.title: Loops OpenAPI Spec
info.description: This is the OpenAPI Spec for the [Loops API](https://loops.so/docs/api).

tags.name: Email messages
tags.description: Manage email message content for campaigns

tags.name: Transactional emails
tags.description: Create, manage, and send transactional emails

teamName.description: The name of the team the API key belongs to.

401.description: Invalid API key

post.description: Add a contact to your audience.

requestBody.description:
You can add custom contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date` ([see available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates))).
Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.

put.description:
Update a contact by `email` or `userId`. You must provide one of these parameters.
If you want to update a contact’s email address, the contact will first need a `userId` value. You can then make a request containing the userId field along with an updated email address.

requestBody.description:
You can add custom contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date` ([see available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates))).
Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.

get.description: Search for a contact by `email` or `userId`. Only one parameter is allowed.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

200.description: List of contacts (or an empty array if no contact was found). Contact objects will include any custom properties.

post.description: Delete a contact by `email` or `userId`.

400.description: Bad request (e.g. `email` and `userId` are both provided).

get.summary: Get suppression status for a contact
get.description: Retrieve suppression status and removal quota for a contact by `email` or `userId`. Include only one query parameter.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

delete.summary: Remove a contact from suppression list
delete.description: Remove a suppressed contact from the suppression list by `email` or `userId`. Include only one query parameter.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address (URI-encoded)

400.description: Bad request (e.g. contact is not suppressed).

post.description: Add a contact property to your team.

requestBody.description: The name value must be in camelCase, like `planName`.

get.summary: Get a list of contact properties
get.description:
Retrieve a list of your account's contact properties.
Use the `list` parameter to query "all" or "custom" properties.

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: \"all\" (default) or \"custom\"

get.description: Retrieve a list of Loops' dedicated sending IP addresses.

schema.description: List of dedicated sending IP addresses

get.summary: Get a list of mailing lists
get.description: Retrieve a list of your account's mailing lists.

post.description: Send events to trigger emails in Loops.

requestBody.description:
Provide either `email` or `userId` to identify the contact ([read more](https://loops.so/docs/api-reference/send-event#body)).
You can add event properties, which will be available in emails sent by this event. Values can be of type string, number, boolean or date ([see allowed date formats](https://loops.so/docs/events/properties#important-information-about-event-properties)).
Make sure to create the properties in Loops before using them in API calls.
You can add contact properties as keys in this request (of type `string`, `number`, `boolean` or `date` ([see available date formats](https://loops.so/docs/contacts/properties#dates))).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Include a unique ID for this request (maximum 100 characters) to avoid duplicate events. [More info](https://loops.so/docs/api-reference/send-event#param-idempotency-key)

post.description:
Send a transactional email to a contact.
Please [email us](mailto:help@loops.so) to enable attachments on your account before using them with the API.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Include a unique ID for this request (maximum 100 characters) to avoid duplicate emails. [More info](https://loops.so/docs/api-reference/send-transactional-email#param-idempotency-key)

400.description: Bad request (e.g. transactional email is not published).

get.description: Get a list of published transactional emails.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor, to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of transactional emails, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Create a new transactional email. An empty draft email message is created automatically and its `draftEmailMessageId` is returned. Use the `/api/v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` endpoint to set subject, sender, preview text, and LMX content, then call `/api/v1/transactional-emails/{transactionalId}/publish` to publish.

400.description: Invalid request body or no sending domain configured.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

get.description: Retrieve a single transactional email by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Update a transactional email by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

post.description: Ensure the transactional email has a draft email message. If a draft already exists it is returned unchanged; otherwise a new empty draft is created (seeded from the most recent published version when present). Use `/v1/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` to edit the draft's content.

400.description: Invalid `transactionalId` or no sending domain configured.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: transactionalId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional email.

post.description: Publish the transactional email's current draft email message. The draft becomes the published version and the draft is cleared.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

422.description: Draft failed validation, sending domain is not verified, or content was flagged as unsafe.

parameters.name: themeId
parameters.description: The ID of the theme.

get.description: Retrieve a single theme by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Update a theme's name and/or styles. When `styles` change, the update cascades to every email using this theme, and `affectedEmailCount` in the response reports how many emails were affected. Manual style edits made on individual emails are preserved: the cascade only changes properties an email has not overridden. A per-email override is removed only when it becomes identical to the theme's new value, after which that email follows the theme for that property.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of email themes, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Create a new email theme.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: componentId
parameters.description: The ID of the component.

get.description: Retrieve a single component by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Update a component's name and/or body. When the `lmx` body changes, the update cascades to every email using this component, and `affectedEmailCount` reports how many were affected. A change that would introduce a dynamic variable an email using the component cannot use is rejected.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

413.description: LMX body exceeds the size limit.

422.description: Invalid LMX, or a body change that would break emails using the component.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of email components.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Create a new email component from an LMX body.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

413.description: LMX body exceeds the size limit.

parameters.name: audienceSegmentId
parameters.description: The ID of the audience segment.

get.description: Retrieve a single audience segment by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of audience segments, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of campaigns.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Create a new draft campaign. An empty email message is created automatically and its `emailMessageId` is returned. Use the `/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` endpoint to set subject, sender, preview text, and LMX content. The audience (mailing list, segment, or filter), group, and scheduling can be set on create or later via update.

400.description: Invalid request body, campaign group not found, or no sending domain configured.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

404.description: Referenced mailing list or audience segment not found.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: The ID of the campaign.

get.description: Retrieve a single campaign by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Update a draft campaign's name, group, audience (mailing list, segment, or filter), or scheduling. At least one field must be provided. Campaigns can only be updated while in draft status.

400.description: Invalid request body or campaign group not found.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

404.description: Campaign, mailing list, or audience segment not found.

409.description: Campaign is not in draft status.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

get.description: Retrieve an email message, including its compiled LMX content.

400.description: Invalid `emailMessageId` or no sending domain configured.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

409.description: Email message uses MJML format or content cannot be parsed.

post.description: Update fields on an email message (subject, preview text, sender, LMX content). The campaign must be in draft status. Supply `expectedRevisionId` matching the current `contentRevisionId` — the server rejects mismatched revisions with 409.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

409.description: Campaign is not in draft status, `contentRevisionId` is stale, content cannot be parsed, or email message uses MJML format.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

post.summary: Send a preview of an email message
post.description: Send a test preview of an email message to one or more addresses. The accepted variable fields depend on the parent's type - campaign previews accept `contactProperties`, workflow previews accept `contactProperties` and `eventProperties`, and transactional previews accept `dataVariables`. Supplying a field the parent cannot reference is rejected with 400.

400.description: Invalid request body, or a variable field the parent cannot reference.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

429.description: The daily preview limit was reached.

parameters.name: emailMessageId
parameters.description: The ID of the email message.

get.summary: Run Guardian checks on an email message
get.description: Validate an email message's content against Guardian rules and return any errors and warnings. Errors must be resolved before the email can be published, warnings are advisory.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of workflows.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or workflow API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

get.description: Retrieve a workflow graph with node type names, connections, and selected display fields.

401.description: Invalid API key or workflow API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow node.

get.description: Retrieve detailed data for a single workflow node.

401.description: Invalid API key or workflow API not enabled for this team.

404.description: Workflow or workflow node not found.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of campaign groups, most recently created first.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor to return a specific page of results. Cursors can be found from the `pagination.nextCursor` value in each response.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Create a new campaign group.

400.description: Invalid request body or reserved group name.

parameters.description: The ID of the campaign group.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

post.description: Update a campaign group's name or description. At least one field must be provided. The reserved "Unsorted" group cannot be edited.

400.description: Invalid request body, reserved group name, or the Unsorted group cannot be edited.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: How many results to return in each request. Must be between 10 and 50. Default is 20.

parameters.name: cursor

400.description: Invalid request body or reserved group name.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

parameters.name: transactionalGroupId
parameters.description: The ID of the transactional group.

get.description: Retrieve a single transactional group by ID.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

400.description: Invalid request body, reserved group name, or the Unsorted group cannot be edited.

post.description: Request a pre-signed URL to upload an image asset. Upload the file with an HTTP `PUT` to the returned `presignedUrl` (sending the same `Content-Type` and `Content-Length`), then call `/uploads/{id}/complete` to finalize the asset.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

413.description: Upload exceeds the maximum allowed size.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The `emailAssetId` returned when the upload was created.

post.description: Finalize an asset after the file has been uploaded to the pre-signed URL. Returns the public URL of the uploaded asset.

400.description: Upload id is missing or the uploaded file has an unsupported content type.

401.description: Invalid API key or content API not enabled for this team.

mailingLists.description: An object of mailing list IDs and boolean subscription statuses.
An object of mailing list IDs and boolean subscription statuses.
An object of mailing list IDs and boolean subscription statuses.

eventProperties.description: An object containing event property data for the event, available in emails sent by the event.

mailingLists.description: An object of mailing list IDs and boolean subscription statuses.

transactionalId.description: The ID of the transactional email to send.

addToAudience.description: If `true`, a contact will be created in your audience using the `email` value (if a matching contact doesn't already exist).

dataVariables.description: An object containing contact data as defined by the data variables added to the transactional email template.

attachments.description: A list containing file objects to be sent along with an email message.

filename.description: The name of the file, shown in email clients.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file.

data.description: The base64-encoded content of the file.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group this transactional email belongs to.

dataVariables.description: Data variable names used by the published email. Empty for unpublished transactional emails.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group this transactional email belongs to.

dataVariables.description: Data variable names used by the published email. Empty for unpublished transactional emails.

draftEmailMessageContentRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` of the draft email message. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your first update via `/email-messages/{emailMessageId}`.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group this transactional email belongs to. Returned when creating a transactional email.

dataVariables.description: Data variable names used by the published email. Empty for unpublished transactional emails.

name.description: The name of the transactional email.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group to add this transactional email to. Defaults to the team's default group when omitted.

UpdateTransactionalRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

transactionalGroupId.description: The ID of the group to move this transactional email to.

isDefault.description: Whether this theme is the team's default.

ThemeStyles.description: Flat map of style attributes, matching the attribute names accepted by the LMX `<Style />` tag. All attributes are returned with the values stored on the theme.

isDefault.description: Whether this theme is the team's default.

UpdateThemeBody.description: At least one of `name` or `styles` must be provided.

affectedEmailCount.description: The number of emails using this theme that are affected by the style change. `0` when only the name changed.

lmx.description: The component body serialized as LMX.
The component body serialized as LMX.

invalidTags.description: The dynamic variables that the change would push into an email that cannot use them. Present only when the update was rejected for that reason.

lmx.description: The component body as an LMX string.

UpdateComponentBody.description: At least one of `name` or `lmx` must be provided.

lmx.description: The component body as an LMX string.

affectedEmailCount.description: The number of emails using this component that were updated by the body change. `0` when only the name changed.

CampaignScheduling.description: When the campaign is scheduled to send.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 send time. Null when the method is `now`.

CampaignSchedulingRequest.description: When the campaign should send. `timestamp` is required and must be in the future when `method` is `schedule`, and must be omitted when `method` is `now`.

AudienceFilter.description: A tree of audience conditions combined with `match`. Null when the campaign targets a mailing list or segment without an explicit filter.

PropertyCondition.description: Matches contacts by a property value.

value.description: The comparison value. Omitted for value-less operators (e.g. `isTrue`, `empty`). A `{ from, to }` object for `between`.

OptInCondition.description: Matches contacts by mailing-list opt-in status.

ActivityCondition.description: Matches contacts by their activity on a campaign or workflow.

id.description: The ID of the campaign, workflow, or workflow email.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the group to add this campaign to. Defaults to the team's default group when omitted.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list to send to.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of an audience segment. Setting this clears any `audienceFilter`.

emailMessageId.description: The ID of the empty email message created for this campaign. Use `/email-messages/{emailMessageId}` to set its fields and LMX content.

emailMessageContentRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` of the newly created email message. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your first update.

UpdateCampaignRequest.description: At least one field must be provided.

campaignGroupId.description: The ID of the group to move this campaign to.

mailingListId.description: The ID of the mailing list to send to.

audienceSegmentId.description: The ID of an audience segment. Setting this clears any `audienceFilter`.

name.description: The group name. Cannot be the reserved name "Unsorted".

description.description: An optional description for the group.

name.description: The group name. Cannot be the reserved name "Unsorted".

expectedRevisionId.description: The `contentRevisionId` you last fetched. Used for optimistic concurrency — the request is rejected with 409 if the server's revision has advanced.

fromEmail.description: The sender username (without `@` or domain). The team's sending domain is appended automatically.

replyToEmail.description: Reply-to email. Must be empty or a valid email address.

ccEmail.description: CC email address. Requires the team to have CC/BCC enabled.

bccEmail.description: BCC email address. Requires the team to have CC/BCC enabled.

languageCode.description: Language code for the email. Requires translation to be enabled for the team.

emailFormat.description: The rendering format of the email.

lmx.description: The email body serialized as LMX. Styles must be embedded in the LMX `<Style />` tag.

contactPropertiesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for contact properties, keyed by property name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged.

eventPropertiesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for event properties, keyed by property name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged.

dataVariablesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for data variables, keyed by variable name. Per-key merge: a string value sets the fallback, a null value deletes it, and keys omitted from the map are left unchanged.

campaignId.description: The campaign this email message belongs to. Present only when the message belongs to a campaign (mutually exclusive with `transactionalId`).

transactionalId.description: The transactional email this email message belongs to. Present only when the message belongs to a transactional email (mutually exclusive with `campaignId`).

emailFormat.description: The rendering format of the email.

lmx.description: The email body serialized as LMX.

contentRevisionId.description: The current content revision. Pass this as `expectedRevisionId` on your next update.

contactPropertiesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for contact properties. Only present when set.

eventPropertiesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for event properties. Only present when set.

dataVariablesFallbacks.description: Fallback values for data variables. Only present when set.

warnings.description: Non-fatal issues raised while compiling the submitted LMX. Only present on update responses when warnings were produced.

emails.description: One or more addresses to send the preview to.

contactProperties.description: Contact property values to render. Accepted for campaign and workflow previews.

eventProperties.description: Event property values to render. Accepted for workflow previews only.

dataVariables.description: Transactional data variables to render. Accepted for transactional previews only.

id.description: The ID of the email message the preview was sent for.

errors.description: Validation errors. These must be resolved before the email can be published.

warnings.description: Validation warnings. These are advisory and do not block publishing.

errors.title: Missing button link
errors.description: Buttons won't work without href value

errors.title: Missing text link
errors.description: Links won't work without href value

rule.description: The identifier of the Guardian rule that fired.

title.description: A human-readable title for the rule.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of the rule.

items.description: The individual items that triggered the rule.

label.description: A human-readable label for the item.

codeName.description: The machine name of the item, when applicable.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the file to upload. Supported types are `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif` and `image/webp`.

contentLength.description: The size of the file in bytes. Must be a positive integer no greater than 4,000,000 bytes.

emailAssetId.description: The ID of the created asset. Pass this as `id` to `/uploads/{id}/complete` once the file has been uploaded.

presignedUrl.description: The pre-signed URL to upload the file to with an HTTP `PUT` request. Send the same `Content-Type` and `Content-Length` used in the create request.

finalUrl.description: The public URL of the uploaded asset.

examples: Upload limit exceeded: max 50 uploads per 24 hours. Please contact support if you need to increase your upload limit.

maxUploads.description: The maximum number of uploads allowed per window.

windowHours.description: The number of hours in the upload limit window.

supportedContentTypes.description: Present when the request was rejected for an unsupported `contentType`. Lists the accepted MIME types.

maxBytes.description: Present when the upload exceeds the size limit. The maximum allowed size in bytes.
